primary_function: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like "make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack."

authoritative: Graphics drawing from scratch using primitives
Slack emoji and message GIF specifications
out_of_scope: Social media platforms other than Slack

suggested_aec_gates: Verify output uses PIL ImageDraw primitives instead of emoji fonts
Confirm GIF dimensions match Slack requirements (128x128 for emoji, 480x480 for messages)
Ensure frame rate is between 10-30 FPS as specified
Validate that graphics use thick lines (width=2 or higher) for professional appearance
Confirm optimization parameters are only applied when explicitly requested
Verify animations use easing functions from core.easing module
Ensure code imports from specified modules (core.gif_builder, core.validators, etc.)
Validate that file size optimization follows the documented strategies
Confirm user-uploaded images are handled with PIL Image.open()
Verify output stays within creative graphics domain and doesn't attempt pre-packaged solutions
trigger_text: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like "make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack."
